mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20d returns in more than 65% of instances when unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60d returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases are followed by negative 5d returns in more than 60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in more than 58% of periods when CPI inflation exceeds the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields negative 20d returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals economic contraction and reduced corporate profit margins, which systematically depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth is followed by positive 60d returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 60% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
